en: You're right. Maybe Madeline wants to inform us all that she's a Chooksan. A what?
ko: 맞아. 아마 마들린이 우리가 그녀가 축산이라고 알리려는 것 같아. 축산이 뭐야?

en: A Chooksan. A Korean toilet ghost.
Lives in an outhouse, wraps her hair around your throat, and chokes you to death while you move your bowels?
ko: 외양간에 살고, 머리카락으로 목을 감아 죽일 때까지 질식시키는 거야?

en: You know what? I will give you $6,000 if the announcement is she's a Chooksan.
ko: 알겠어. 만약 발표가 그녀가 축산이라고 하면 6,000달러 줄게.

en: Okay, everyone. I have some news.
That Madeline Wunsch is a Korean toilet ghost? Boring. We already knew that.
ko: 마들린 원쉬가 한국 화장실 귀신이라고? 지루해. 우리는 이미 알고 있었어.

en: Madeline Wunsch is dead. Say what now?
Yeah. The commissioner's office just notified us.
No way that's true. As Wunsch says when she sees deodorant, I'm not buying it. Sir, she's dead.
ko: 그게 사실일 리 없어. 원쉬가 데오도란트를 보면 말하듯이, 난 믿지 않아. 그녀는 죽었어.
오, 테리. 좀비는 죽을 수 없어.

en: This is some sort of scam. If she were dead, we would be hearing the sounds of children singing in the streets.
ko: 이건 어떤 사기야. 만약 그녀가 죽었다면, 거리에서 아이들이 노래하는 소리를 듣고 있을 거야.

en: I don't know, sir. You didn't believe it when we told you the disco strangler died either. And I was right. He was faking it. Maybe she's alive.
ko: 잘 모르겠어, 선생님. 우리가 디스코 교살자가 죽었다고 말했을 때도 믿지 않았잖아. 그리고 내가 맞았어. 그는 가짜였어. 아마 그녀도 살아있을 거야.

en: Why would Wunsch fake her own death?
The same reason she visits Mexico once a year and sucks the blood from all the goats for kicks.
ko: 매년 한 번 멕시코에 가서 염소의 피를 빨아먹기 위해서야.

en: Look, I don't know what to tell you, but she's dead.
What do you want? To open up her coffin and check for yourself? Oh, she's very dead.
ko: 뭘 원해? 그녀의 관을 열어보고 확인해? 오, 그녀는 정말 죽었어.
